creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
This port does not build. Looks like the new IO scheduling feature
doesn't work on FreeBSD.

cc -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -I.  -DNDEBUG -I/usr/local/include -pedantic -Wall -Wextra -std=c99 -rdynamic -D_XOPEN_SOURCE_EXTENDED -DSYSCONFDIR=\"/usr/local/etc\" -O2 -pipe -I/usr/local/include -fno-strict-aliasing -MT htop-Process.o -MD -MP -MF .deps/htop-Process.Tpo -c -o htop-Process.o `test -f 'Process.c' || echo './'`Process.c
Process.c: In function 'Process_updateIOPriority':
Process.c:607: error: 'SYS_ioprio_get' undeclared (first use in this function)
Process.c:607: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once
Process.c:607: error: for each function it appears in.)
Process.c: In function 'Process_setIOPriority':
Process.c:613: error: 'SYS_ioprio_set' undeclared (first use in this function)
*** [htop-Process.o] Error code 1

I don't have the time to track down a proper fix for this but if you
come up with one please let me know via an update in this PR. Thanks!

-- WXS
